source.description: A reviewed six-panel felt-tip marker process contact sheet.
source.notes: One consistent face-free bowling ball rolling toward three pins on warm paper, with no lettering, logos, lane signage, sticker outline, badge frame, or crowd scene.

process_strategy: The sequence locks one large rolling ball and three pin axes, wraps their silhouettes while preserving the overlap order, adds finger holes and neck bands, introduces motion arcs and a compact impact burst behind the pins, then inks and colors every existing shape before final cleanup only.

final_elements.name: rolling ball circle, three pin axes, and overlap envelopes
final_elements.description: Light construction places one ball and three staggered pin guides, keeping a small paper gap beside the ball while reserving the rear-pin overlaps behind the front-center pin.

final_elements.name: bowling-ball silhouette and three bowling-pin silhouettes
final_elements.description: A round ball and three bottle-shaped pins become readable while the rear pin edges stop cleanly behind the front forms.

final_elements.name: three finger holes, pin neck bands, and pin separation lines
final_elements.description: Three angled finger holes establish the rolling ball orientation while paired neck bands and overlap breaks separate the pins.

final_elements.name: curved motion arcs, small impact burst, and lane baseline
final_elements.description: Two curved motion arcs trail the ball, a compact burst sits behind the pins, and one shallow baseline grounds the action.

final_elements.name: thick black outline with purple, cyan, raspberry, yellow, and white marker fills
final_elements.description: Bold black marker reinforces every established shape; purple fills the ball, cyan accents the holes, raspberry colors the pin bands, and yellow fills the impact burst while pin bodies keep warm paper-white texture.

frames.visible_job: Places a light rolling-ball circle, three staggered pin axes, and overlap envelopes at one fixed scale.
introduces: rolling ball circle, three pin axes, and overlap envelopes

frames.visible_job: Wraps the guides with one round ball and three readable pin silhouettes, preserving the planned front-to-back overlaps.
introduces: bowling-ball silhouette and three bowling-pin silhouettes
requires_prior_elements: rolling ball circle, three pin axes, and overlap envelopes

frames.visible_job: Adds three angled finger holes, paired pin neck bands, and short separation lines to the established forms.
introduces: three finger holes, pin neck bands, and pin separation lines
requires_prior_elements: bowling-ball silhouette and three bowling-pin silhouettes

frames.visible_job: Adds two curved motion arcs, a compact impact burst behind the pins, and one shallow lane baseline without changing the equipment silhouettes.
introduces: curved motion arcs, small impact burst, and lane baseline
requires_prior_elements: bowling-ball silhouette and three bowling-pin silhouettes
three finger holes, pin neck bands, and pin separation lines

frames.visible_job: Thickens every established contour and fills the ball, holes, pin bands, impact burst, and lane accents with visible marker texture.
introduces: thick black outline with purple, cyan, raspberry, yellow, and white marker fills
requires_prior_elements: bowling-ball silhouette and three bowling-pin silhouettes
three finger holes, pin neck bands, and pin separation lines
curved motion arcs, small impact burst, and lane baseline

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens the established ball, pin overlaps, finger holes, neck bands, motion arcs, impact burst, baseline, marker fills, and highlight gaps.
allowed_changes: small highlight gaps on established shapes

rejection_checks: No ball, pin, finger hole, neck band, separation line, motion arc, impact burst, baseline, outline, or color area may first appear in the final panel.
The ball and exactly three pins must retain one overlap order, scale, crop, and action angle across every frame.
Do not finish rear-pin edges hidden behind the front-center pin; keep all three heads, necks, outer sides, and bases clearly countable.
Reject faces, text, logos, scores, extra pins, bowling shoes, crowds, sticker outlines, badge frames, or decorative borders.
Reject glossy vector icon art, airbrushed poster rendering, smooth digital gradients, or mechanically perfect symmetry.
